results.title: Ladies in lines
results.brief_description: Depicts women recruits in the Australian Army training camp at Kapooka. The video follows the women as they arrive at Kapooka, to the shouting abuse of the army corporals. The second week of training is marked by strenuous exercise. Shot over the full twelve weeks of their basic training course, this documentary is a graphically accurate portrayal of the process of military indoctrination which tests the women both physically and mentally. Although they came from both urban and country environments, the recruits were united by their desire to make something of their lives. In the end, many were forced to ask themselves the question, ‘is it worth it?’ Frank, compelling and controversial, this is award-winning documentary making at its finest.

results.title: Labrador Hospital
results.creator_credit: Kryn Taconis, Rex Tasker and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: A documentary study of the Grenfell Mission, a voluntary medical group that has served Atlantic fishermen and the isolated communities of Newfoundland and Labrador since 1892. Focus of the film is the Mission’s headquarters hospital at St. Anthony and the modern system of airlifting up to 5000 patients a year to hospital.

results.title: Labour of Love
results.creator_credit: Harriet Clutterbuck, Martyn Down, Imago and Pumpkin Films
results.brief_description: Demonstrates the positive aspects of home childbirth following the experiences of several families. Interviews their midwives about the advantages of having a baby amongst family and friends in familiar surroundings.

results.title: Labor in power
results.creator_credit: Sue Spencer and Australian Broadcasting Corporation
results.brief_description: In a decade of profound change, from 1983 to 1993, the ABC Television News and Current Affairs Political Documentary Unit compiled a unique series, capturing the key elements of the Australian Labour Party’s ten-year rule. The battle between Bob Hawke and Paul Keating for the Labor Party leadership was one of the most divisive in Australian political history the seeds of which can be traced back as far as 1980. The series is an intensely personal examination of the Hawke-Keating relationship, and through more than 120 interviews with Cabinet Ministers, senior bureaucrats and staff advisers, the series offers an insider’s view of who wields power in Australia, and how. The five episodes are; “Taking power”, “Taxing times”, “Conserving power”, “The recession we had to have” and “The sweetest victory”.

results.title: Labirynt
results.creator_credit: Jan Lenica, Studio Miniatur Filmowych and Warszawie Przedstawia Film
results.brief_description: Jan Lenica’s diverse animation techniques in this film create a cruel and absurd world from which escape is impossible - anyone caught up in this Kafka-like nightmare can only persist, undeterred.

results.title: La Sylphide
results.brief_description: La Sylphide, in diaphanous white costume and with shining wings, dances for the Scottish farmer James, in the first scene of this most famous and enduring ballet by August Bournonville. This production by the Royal Dutch Ballet was partly recorded in the atmosphere of live performance but essentially in the confines of an empty Royal Theatre. The orchestra is conducted by Paul Jorgensen.

results.title: La Sel de la terre
results.creator_credit: Georges Rouquier, V. Blavier and Intermondia Films
results.brief_description: Lyrical impression of the Camargue county at the mouth of the Rhone - long, flat reaches of salt impregnated land, rich in bird and animal life. The latter sequences deal with the draining of the land its fertilization and bringing it into production. Sparse commentary in French.

results.title: La scalo lo scalone
results.brief_description: A child born to Italian parents in country Victoria learns about his growing sexuality. In innocence he and his friends play the usual child games of Doctors and Nurses as a natural means of learning. His play is fettered by religious guilt (that he’s yet to understand) and further complicated my his mother’s sudden death. A story about the loss of innocence - both as a state of mind and a time in one’s life that will never be the same again.

results.title: La Poulette grise (TEFC)
results.brief_description: Presentation in cartoon form by Norman McLaren of an old French-Canadian folk song. Anna Malenfant sings the traditional French-Canadian song about the little grey hen who nests in the moon, the words of the song being illustrated by animated forms.

results.title: La Poulette grise
results.brief_description: A Norman McLaren lullaby on film about the little grey hen who nests in the church, the little brown hen who nests in the moon, and others. Photographed from pastel drawings, the transient images express the dreamy mood of this old French song.
